name: Villanueva de Córdoba - Virgen de Luna Sanctuary Loop
name_original: Villanueva de Córdoba, Santuario de la Virgen de Luna – ruta circular
description: This moderate 29.9-mile mountain bike loop in Los Pedroches offers diverse terrain, scenic holm oak groves, and the historic Virgen de Luna
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villanueva De Córdoba offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Morena mountains, characterized by the expansive *dehesa* landscape. This region features Europe's largest holm oak pastureland, providing a mix of open pastures and ancient oak forests. The undulating terrain includes gentle slopes and varied rock formations, making it suitable for exploring the natural environment. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Córdoba The most popular mountain bike trail is Villanueva de Córdoba - Virgen de Luna Sanctuary Loop, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the dehesa, offering views of the unique agroforestry system. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sanctuary of the Virgin of Luna – Sanctuary of the Virgin loop from Villanueva de Córdoba, a moderate 24.4 miles (39.2 km) path. This route explores the natural surroundings of the sanctuary, featuring a mix of open and wooded sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Pozoviejo Square – El Quijote Sculpture loop from Alabardero, a 37.9 miles (61.1 km) trail leading through the dehesa and rural landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Villanueva De Córdoba is defined by expansive dehesa, ancient holm oaks, and varied rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villanueva De Córdoba?
A: There are 9 mountain bike trails documented around Villanueva De Córdoba on komoot, offering a range of experiences through the region's unique dehesa landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villanueva De Córdoba?
A: The terrain around Villanueva De Córdoba is primarily characterized by the expansive *dehesa* landscape, Europe's largest holm oak pastureland. You'll find a mix of open pastures, ancient oak forests, and undulating terrain with gentle slopes. Some routes may feature varied rock formations and more challenging sections, reflecting the Sierra Morena Cordobesa's natural beauty.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Villanueva De Córdoba?
A: While there are no trails explicitly marked as 'easy' in the current selection, many routes through the dehesa are described as moderate and non-competitive, such as the local 'Ruta de Ciclismo por la Dehesa – BTT NaturCOR'. These routes focus on enjoying the environment, making them suitable for those with some cycling experience or families looking for a scenic ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988345654/villanueva-de-cordoba-virgen-de-luna-sanctuary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Córdoba - Virgen de Luna Sanctuary Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through the stunning dehesa landscape, offering picturesque views of holm and cork oaks. You can also find attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4288368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalmellato Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadalmellato Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Technical Descent – Villanueva de Córdoba loop from Villanueva de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 71.8 km trail with significant elevation changes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Los Pedroches – Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna loop from Villanueva de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; offers another difficult option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villanueva De Córdoba?
A: The Sierra Morena region, including Villanueva De Córdoba, is generally pleasant for outdoor activities during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, so checking seasonal conditions is advisable. Spring offers lush landscapes, while autumn showcases beautiful colors in the dehesa.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Córdoba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988345654/villanueva-de-cordoba-virgen-de-luna-sanctuary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Córdoba - Virgen de Luna Sanctuary Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozoviejo Square – El Quijote Sculpture loop from Alabardero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in the dehesa?
A: The dehesa landscape is rich in biodiversity. You might encounter deer, wild boars, and various bird species, including the imperial eagle. The region is also home to the elusive Iberian lynx, though sightings are rare.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Villanueva De Córdoba?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Villanueva De Córdoba is a town that promotes outdoor activities. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town, providing access to the trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What other outdoor activities can be enjoyed in the Villanueva De Córdoba area besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, the natural setting of Villanueva De Córdoba and the Sierra Morena offers excellent opportunities for hiking through the dehesa and forests. Nearby reservoirs and rivers, such as the Guadalmellato, are suitable for water activities like kayaking. Horse riding is also a popular way to explore the pastoral landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, some routes pass by or near significant landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4288368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building often included in loop routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España in Villanueva de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is also a central historical site within the town itself.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Villanueva De Córdoba?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails varies significantly based on their length and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988345654/villanueva-de-cordoba-virgen-de-luna-sanctuary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Córdoba - Virgen de Luna Sanctuary Loop&lt;/a&gt; (48.1 km) can take around 4 hours, while longer and more difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Technical Descent – Villanueva de Córdoba loop&lt;/a&gt; (71.8 km), may require over 6 hours to complete.
tips: Located in the Valle de Los Pedroches region, the town has several places of architectural interest: its streets, manor houses, corners and, above all, the perfect combination of granite, a typical construction material of the region, and limestone. Highlights are the Church of San Miguel and the Old Palace of Justice.
A particularly beautiful section. There are curves in the path, a good surface, holm oaks, and stone walls that cross the section.

The Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna is one of the largest in the region. Pozoblanco and Villanueva de Córdoba share this chapel, making each of the towns a different pilgrimage on different dates.

At the back of the hermitage there is a public toilet.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain, including approximately 23.2 km of unpaved roads, 18.1 km of asphalt, 5.51 km of natural trails, and 4.71 km of paved roads. You'll encounter 'punchy single track climbs' with 'lots of rocks and loose stuff,' especially in technical sections. The path also winds through meadows with distinctive 'granite walls' and natural holm oak groves (dehesa).

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several notable features. In Villanueva de Córdoba, you can see the Church of San Miguel and Old Courthouse. A significant highlight is the Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna, an emblematic religious site nestled within the Dehesa de la Jara. The route also includes a Scenic Trail Between Granite Walls.

faqs.question: How difficult is this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This loop is generally classified as 'moderate,' but sections can feel 'hard' depending on your fitness and technical skills. Expect 'savage climbs' and 'tough single track' with steep gradients. It can be a 'really big day' with significant effort required. If you're looking for a less strenuous experience on the more challenging parts, an e-bike is a good option.

faqs.question: Is the Villanueva de Córdoba - Virgen de Luna Sanctuary Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the presence of livestock in the dehesa landscape, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas with cattle or other animals, to ensure their safety and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride is typically during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, making the ride more challenging, while winters can bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike loop. The trail passes through public access areas within the Dehesa de la Jara and the Los Pedroches Valley.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Villanueva de Córdoba, near the starting point of the loop. There is also parking available closer to the Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna, which can serve as an alternative access point for the loop.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Camino de la Virgen de Luna, Camino de Pedroche a la Virgen de la Luna, Camino de la Báscula, Camino de Góngora, Camino de las Mojoneras, and Paseo de Andalucía.

faqs.question: Are there public facilities available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find public toilets conveniently located at the back of the Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna, which is a key highlight on the loop.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the ride?
faqs.answer: The loop takes you through the stunning Dehesa de la Jara, part of the Los Pedroches Valley, known for being 'the best preserved meadow landscape in all of Europe.' You'll enjoy beautiful views between ancient holm oak trees and 'impressive views' from elevated points, showcasing the unique dehesa landscape.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given the 'moderate' to 'hard' difficulty, with 'savage climbs' and technical sections, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It requires good fitness and some advanced mountain biking skills. More experienced families might enjoy it, but be prepared for a challenging ride.
